serviceNotes.engine oil: Mercedes-Benz EU maintenance booklets for W210 diesel models specify engine oil and filter replacement at fixed distance and time limits (typically 15,000 km or 12 months under the common European fixed-interval sheet, whichever comes first). ASSYST may allow longer intervals on some vehicles; follow the in-dash service indicator if present.
serviceNotes.dust filter: Mercedes-Benz uses the term dust filter (Staubfilter / interior dust filter) in service documentation for the cabin air filter element. Replacement is typically required at major service visits (often every 2 years or 30,000 km on fixed plans).
serviceNotes.engine air filter: Engine air filter replacement interval follows Mercedes inspection schedules (commonly every 60,000 km or 48 months on EU fixed maintenance tables for this era).
serviceNotes.brake fluid: Mercedes-Benz specifies brake fluid replacement on a time basis (typically every 24 months) for vehicles of this generation; brake fluid is hygroscopic and must not be assigned a distance-only interval.
serviceNotes.check brake pad thickness: Brake pad wear is inspected at each scheduled maintenance visit; a 30,000 km reminder aligns with alternating EU service visits when oil service is at 15,000 km.
serviceNotes.fuel filter: Diesel fuel filter replacement is part of the manufacturer maintenance programme (commonly around 60,000 km on OM611/OM613-era schedules; verify against your printed service booklet for the exact engine code).
serviceNotes.coolant: Mercedes-Benz specifies nitrite-free coolant with a long change interval on W210 diesels; many EU booklists quote coolant renewal around 160,000 km—confirm in the owner’s maintenance booklet.
serviceNotes.automatic transmission fluid: 722.6 five-speed automatics are often labelled fill-for-life officially; specialists recommend fluid and filter service around 60,000 km for long-term reliability.
serviceNotes.manual transmission oil: Gear oil is typically treated as long-life; renewal around 100,000 km is a common workshop recommendation for Mercedes manual gearboxes.
serviceNotes.coolant flush: If the OEM long interval is not yet due, a full coolant exchange around 120,000 km restores corrosion inhibitor protection for the water pump and radiator.
serviceNotes.poly-V belt: Accessory drive belt and tensioner wear with age; preventive replacement around 100,000 km avoids roadside failure.
